c_signature: static int mpfr_sqrt1 (mpfr_ptr r, mpfr_srcptr u, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode)
doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_sqrt1 (mpfr/src/sqrt.c L74-L217). Special-code square-root fast path for prec(r) == prec(u) == p with p < GMP_NUMB_BITS (i.e. 1 <= p <= 63 on a 64-bit GMP). The dispatcher in mpfr_sqrt routes here at mpfr/src/sqrt.c L563-L564 when those conditions hold.

Algorithm: parity-fix the exponent (shift u0 right one bit if exp_u is odd, increment exp_u; otherwise leave); compute exp_r = exp_u / 2; call __gmpfr_sqrt_limb_approx for an integer-sqrt approximation r0 of u0*2^64 within [r0, r0+7]; if the round-bit boundary falls within that 7-ULP window, refine via the explicit Newton fixup loop (L122-L131) until rb*2^64 + sb <= 2*r0; extract round-bit rb and sticky-bit sb at position (sh-1) where sh = 64 - p; mask off the low sh bits of r0 (alignment to p bits); then dispatch to one of the five rounding modes.

The TS port doesn't need any of this complexity: it delegates to the already-shipped src/ops/sqrt.ts (mpfr_sqrt), which uses a bigint isqrt that handles all precs uniformly. The dispatcher condition (prec(u) == prec && prec < 64) means the TS port can safely call mpfr_sqrt(u, prec, rnd) and return its result  --  same value, same ternary, same edge cases. The C reference's __gmpfr_sqrt_limb_approx + fixup loop is a *performance fast path*, not a correctness contract; the contract is identical to the general mpfr_sqrt.

Class is 'transcendental' (1000ms runtime budget) per the standard MPFR classification  --  even though the bigint-delegate implementation is O(1)-ish, the class encodes the function family for the runner's per-test timeout, not the actual complexity.
divergence_from_c:
Five divergences:

  1. Return shape: C mutates `r` in place and returns int ternary. TS takes (u, prec, rnd) and returns Result {value, ternary} per src/core.ts L173-L176. The (prec, rnd) reflect what C reads off `r` (its precision and the rnd_mode arg); since the dispatcher precondition is prec(r) == prec(u), the TS port can assume u.prec == prec.

  2. Dispatcher precondition: C is only called from mpfr_sqrt when prec(r) == prec(u) AND 1 <= prec(u) <= 63 (mpfr/src/sqrt.c L561-L564). The TS port enforces the SAME precondition as a defensive check at function entry: throws MPFRError('EPREC', ...) if prec(u) != prec OR prec >= 64. The golden driver ONLY emits cases that satisfy the precondition, so a correct port never hits the throw branch on golden inputs.

  3. Singular dispatch: C is unreachable for NaN/Inf/zero/negative inputs (the public mpfr_sqrt dispatches those at L522-L554 before reaching the prec-window fast paths). The TS port likewise assumes u is kind='normal' with sign=+1; on any other input it can either delegate to mpfr_sqrt (which handles all singulars correctly) OR throw  --  the recommended path is delegate, since the wire form has no need to distinguish 'this driver only generates valid inputs' from 'singulars are documented as unreachable'.

  4. Underflow/overflow side effects: C calls mpfr_underflow / mpfr_overflow which mutate __gmpfr_flags (the global flag register). The TS port omits the flag side effect per the locked-schema policy (src/core.ts has no flag-state API). The returned value is still correct under exp-overflow/underflow because mpfr_sqrt's algebraic core handles the exponent computation directly; the C side's flag setting is a side channel the TS port doesn't expose.

  5. Algorithm: C uses __gmpfr_sqrt_limb_approx + an explicit Newton fixup loop. TS delegates to bigint isqrt via src/ops/sqrt.ts. Both produce the same correctly-rounded sqrt for any (u, prec, rnd) triple  --  the C path is faster (single-limb integer ops); the TS path is shorter (one bigint isqrt). Byte-identical output on every case.

ADR 0001 application:
  - signature.returns: gen_spec emitted 'Result' (correctly mapped from C int via _classify_return). Curator confirms  --  this is the canonical idiomatic-TS lift for ops that return ternary alongside a value. (ADR 0001: signature.returns curator-wins, but curator agrees here.)
  - signature.params: gen_spec emitted ['u', 'prec', 'rnd'] (correctly inferred from the (u, rnd) C signature + the implicit prec from MPFR_GET_PREC(r)). Curator confirms. (ADR 0001: signature.params curator-wins, curator agrees.)
  - class: gen_spec did not assign (file-stem heuristic would give 'misc' or 'transcendental' depending on rule). Curator override: 'transcendental' per the standard MPFR classification + 1000ms runtime budget. (ADR 0001: class curator-wins.)
  - prec_unit: gen_spec emitted 'bits' (default). Curator confirms  --  prec is meaningful, units are bits.
refs: mpfr/src/sqrt.c L561-L564 -- dispatcher routes here when prec(r) == prec(u) AND prec < GMP_NUMB_BITS.
mpfr/src/invsqrt_limb.h -- __gmpfr_sqrt_limb_approx, the single-limb sqrt approximation that seeds the fixup loop. TS port doesn't need it (bigint isqrt is exact).
src/ops/sqrt.ts -- the unified TS mpfr_sqrt that the port delegates to.

eval/functions/mpfr_div_2/spec.json -- structural precedent: standalone wire form for a static helper that the unified TS port doesn't actually delegate to, justified by class-coverage and dispatcher-correctness testing.
mpfr/tests/tsqrt.c -- source for mined cases (small-prec sqrt patterns).
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: Correct rounding does not mean unique output' -- the dispatcher routing is a perf detail; correctness is uniquely determined by (u, prec, rnd).
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: Ternary flag is the sign of (rounded - exact)' -- C returns ternary directly; the TS port preserves the direction.
docs/reports/010-shadow-trial.md -- shadow-trial precedent for the static-helper wire-form pattern.
